---
title: "BEAR"
subtitle: "Benchmarks of Empirical Accuracy in Research"
---

BEAR is a meta-database of z-values, effect sizes, and standard errors
from existing datasets across scientific fields. It does not contribute
new source data; it repackages public datasets into a common structure
for research on replication, exchangeability, meta-analysis, and related
questions about empirical accuracy.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
The main data artifact is distributed through
[GitHub Releases](https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR/releases), not through
this website.
:::

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## What is included?

BEAR combines curated single-study datasets, collections of
meta-analyses, large text-mined biomedical datasets, clinical-trial
databases, and direct replication projects. The current documentation
describes each source dataset, data availability, and the main processing
choices used to bring it into BEAR.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
## Who is this for?

The project is intended for researchers who want a common empirical base
for studying statistical evidence across fields, publication bias,
replicability, power, exchangeability, and the behaviour of meta-analytic
datasets.
:::
:::

## Start here

- [Get data](getting-started.qmd) for release downloads and a minimal R
  loading example.
- [Datasets](datasets.qmd) for the current dataset index and individual
  dataset notes.
- [Artifacts](artifacts.qmd) for the dataset appendix and other public
  documents.
- [Reproduce](reproduce.qmd) for the repository workflow and rebuild
  entry points.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR) for code, issues,
  and releases.
